sections.paragraph:
ταχέως adv. of ταχύς, positive (Hom.+; pap, LXX; En 98:16; TestSol 6:5 D; TestAbr.; JosAs cod. A 16, 2 and ch. 19 [p. 63, 5; 69, 6 Bat.]; ApcMos 21).—Comp. τάχιον ([ταχεῖον Sod. and Vog.; τάχειον t.r. Hippocr., Mul. Morb. 1, 2; Epicurus in Diog. L. 10, 98; Diod S 13, 106, 1; oft. in colloq. speech in general, incl. ViHab 8 [p. 86, 11 Sch.]; Jos., Bell. 5, 537, Ant 2, 142 al.; ins, pap; Wsd 13:9; 1 Macc 2:40; TestIss 6:3. This form was rejected by the Atticists; the Attic form θᾶττον [also 2 Macc; AssMos Fgm. f; Philo, Aet. M. 30; Jos., Ant. 12, 143—WSchmidt 505], which replaced the Homeric θᾶσσον, is found in our lit. only 1 Cl 65:1a; MPol 13:1.—B-D-F §61, 1; 244, 1; W-S. §11, 3; Mlt-H. 164).—Superl. τάχιστα only once (Ac 17:15 s. 1c below), in an expr. taken fr. the literary lang. (B-D-F §60, 2; 244, 1; 453, 4; Rob. 488; 669.—Alcaeus 70, 15 D.2; Menand., Per. 537 S. [287]; Arrian., Anab. 6, 2, 2; Ael. Aristid. 24, 26 K.=44 p. 833 D.; SIG 1168, 4 [IV b.c.]; PSI 360, 12 [252/251 b.c.]; 792, 10; 3 Macc 1:8; TestSol 6:3 D; Jos., Vi. 16). In some instances there may be semantic overlap between mngs. 1 and 2.

① pert. to a very brief extent of time, with focus on speed of action

ⓐ positive

α. in a favorable sense quickly, at once, without delay, soon (Diod S 13, 106, 4; 17, 4, 6; En 98:16; EpArist 291; Jos., Bell. 7, 31; Ant.9, 51) Lk 14:21; 15:22 D; 16:6; J 11:31; B 3:4; AcPl Ha 3, 4; οὓτως ταχέως AcPlCor 2:2 (cp. Gal 1:6 under β).

β. in a remonstrative sense too quickly, too easily, hastily (Pr 25:8; Wsd 14:28; SibOr Fgm. 1, 2) Gal 1:6 (cp. AcPlCor 2:2 under α); 2 Th 2:2; 1 Ti 5:22; Pol 6:1.

ⓑ comp.

α. more quickly, faster Hb 13:19. τάχ. τοῦ Πέτρου faster than Peter J 20:4. The comparison is supplied fr. the context, more quickly (than the others), be the first to … MPol 13:2. θᾶττον ἤ more quickly than 13:1. As quickly, as soon as possible (TestIss 6:3; Ps.-Clem., Hom. 1, 14) 1 Cl 65:1a (θᾶττον) and b (τάχιον). All the more quickly MPol 3:1.

β. without any suggestion of comparison quickly, soon, without delay (PGM 4, 1467 θᾶττον; Jos., Vi. 310 θᾶσσον) J 13:27; difft. Mussies 128: ‘very quickly’; Hm 10, 1, 6.

ⓒ superl. ὡς τάχιστα as soon as possible Ac 17:15.

② pert. to a future point of time that is subsequent to another point of time, with focus on brevity of interval rather than on speed of activity, soon

ⓐ positive 1 Cor 4:19; Phil 2:19 (τ. πέμπ. as Plut., Mor. 612e), 24; 2 Ti 4:9.

ⓑ comp. τάχιον without any suggestion of comparison 1 Ti 3:14 v.l.; Hb 13:23.—DELG s.v. ταχύς. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. ch. = chapter(s)

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Sod. Sod. = HvSoden, ed. Gk. NT—List 1, beg.

Vog. Vog. = HVogels, editor of the Greek NT—List 1, beg.

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

colloq. colloq. = colloquial

incl. incl. = including

ViHab ViHab , s. ViIs

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

AssMos AssMos = Assumption of Moses—List 2

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

expr. expr. = expression

  1. fr. = from

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Alcaeus Alcaeus , lyric poet, VII–VI b.c.—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

difft. difft. = different(ly)

Mussies Mussies = GM., The Morphology of Koine Greek—List 6

Hm Hm = Mandates

superl. superl. = superlative

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 992
